questions.question: boiling In which changes do the particles move further apart? A 1 and 2 B   1 and 3 C   2 and 4 D   3 and 4 2   An atom of element X contains: ●   5 protons ●   6 neutrons ●   5 electrons. Which statements about element X are correct? 1   X has an atomic number of 6. 2   X has a nucleon number of 11. 3   X is in Group II of the Periodic Table. 4   X is in the second period of the Periodic Table., A 1 and 3 B   1 and 4 C   2 and 3 D   2 and 4 3 3 Ammonia, NH 3 , is a covalent molecule. N A H H HN B H H H N C H H HN D H H H 4   Which structure does silicon( IV) oxide most closely resemble?, A carbon dioxide, B diamond, C graphite, D sodium chloride

questions.question: Substance P conducts electricity when solid. Which particles move in solid P so that it can conduct electricity? 1   anions 2   cations 3   electrons A 1 and 2 B 1 only C   2 and 3 D 3 only 4

questions.question: In experiment 1, aqueous copper( II) sulfate is electrolysed using graphite electrodes. In experiment 2, aqueous copper( II) sulfate is electrolysed using copper electrodes. Which statement identifies a half-equation for a reaction at one of the electrodes? A In experiment 1, the half-equation for the anode reaction is 4OH –   2H 2 O  +  O 2 +  4e – ., B In experiment 1, the half-equation for the cathode reaction is 2H + +  2e –   H 2 ., C In experiment 2, the half-equation for the anode reaction is Cu 2+ +  2e –   Cu., D In experiment 2, the half-equation for the cathode reaction is 4OH –   2H 2 O  +  O 2 +  4e – . 6
questions.text: In experiment 1, aqueous copper( II) sulfate is electrolysed using graphite electrodes. In experiment 2, aqueous copper( II) sulfate is electrolysed using copper electrodes. Which statement identifies a half-equation for a reaction at one of the electrodes? A In experiment 1, the half-equation for the anode reaction is 4OH –   2H 2 O  +  O 2 +  4e – ., B In experiment 1, the half-equation for the cathode reaction is 2H + +  2e –   H 2 ., C In experiment 2, the half-equation for the anode reaction is Cu 2+ +  2e –   Cu., D In experiment 2, the half-equation for the cathode reaction is 4OH –   2H 2 O  +  O 2 +  4e – . 6
options: A In experiment 1, the half-equation for the anode reaction is 4OH –   2H 2 O  +  O 2 +  4e – .
B In experiment 1, the half-equation for the cathode reaction is 2H + +  2e –   H 2 .
C In experiment 2, the half-equation for the anode reaction is Cu 2+ +  2e –   Cu.
D In experiment 2, the half-equation for the cathode reaction is 4OH –   2H 2 O  +  O 2 +  4e – . 6

questions.question: Methane burns in excess oxygen. The equation is shown. CH 4 (g)  +  2O 2 (g)    CO 2 (g)  +  2H 2 O(g) Bond energies are shown. bond bond energy in kJ / mol C=O 805 C–H 410 O=O 496 O–H 460 What is the energy change for the reaction? A (4  410 + 2  496) – (2  805 + 4  460), B (2  805 + 4  460) – (4  410 + 2  496), C (410 + 2  496) – (805 + 2  460), D (410 + 496) – (805 + 460)

questions.question: Q and R are elements in the same period of the Periodic Table. Q has 7 electrons in its outer shell and R has 2 electrons in its outer shell. Which statement about Q and R is correct? A Q is a metal and R is a non-metal., B Q and R have different numbers of electron shells., C R is found to the right of Q in the Periodic Table., D The proton number of R is less than the proton number of Q.
questions.text: Q and R are elements in the same period of the Periodic Table. Q has 7 electrons in its outer shell and R has 2 electrons in its outer shell. Which statement about Q and R is correct? A Q is a metal and R is a non-metal., B Q and R have different numbers of electron shells., C R is found to the right of Q in the Periodic Table., D The proton number of R is less than the proton number of Q.
options: A Q is a metal and R is a non-metal.
B Q and R have different numbers of electron shells.
C R is found to the right of Q in the Periodic Table.
D The proton number of R is less than the proton number of Q.

questions.question: A wax candle is made from a mixture of hydrocarbons. Which substance does the cobalt( II) chloride paper identify? A carbon dioxide, B carbon monoxide, C sulfur dioxide, D water

questions.question: The hydrocarbon C 4 H 8 has two structural isomers, but-1-ene and but-2-ene. Which statement is correct? A But-2-ene  has  the  structural  formula  CH 3 CH=CHCH 3 and  the  same  general  formula  as butane., B But-2-ene  has  the  structural  formula  CH 3 CH=CHCH 3 and the same empirical formula as ethene., C But-1-ene  has  the  structural  formula  CH 3 CH 2 CH=CH 2 and  the  same  general  formula  as butane., D But-1-ene  has  the  structural  formula  CH 3 CHCH 2 =CH  and  the  same  empirical  formula  as ethene.
questions.text: The hydrocarbon C 4 H 8 has two structural isomers, but-1-ene and but-2-ene. Which statement is correct? A But-2-ene  has  the  structural  formula  CH 3 CH=CHCH 3 and  the  same  general  formula  as butane., B But-2-ene  has  the  structural  formula  CH 3 CH=CHCH 3 and the same empirical formula as ethene., C But-1-ene  has  the  structural  formula  CH 3 CH 2 CH=CH 2 and  the  same  general  formula  as butane., D But-1-ene  has  the  structural  formula  CH 3 CHCH 2 =CH  and  the  same  empirical  formula  as ethene.
options: A But-2-ene  has  the  structural  formula  CH 3 CH=CHCH 3 and  the  same  general  formula  as butane.
B But-2-ene  has  the  structural  formula  CH 3 CH=CHCH 3 and the same empirical formula as ethene.
C But-1-ene  has  the  structural  formula  CH 3 CH 2 CH=CH 2 and  the  same  general  formula  as butane.
D But-1-ene  has  the  structural  formula  CH 3 CHCH 2 =CH  and  the  same  empirical  formula  as ethene.

questions.question: Rock salt is a mixture of salt and sand. The method used to separate the sand from the salt is listed. step 1   Crush the rock salt, add to warm water and stir. step 2   Pour the mixture through a filter paper held in a funnel. step 3   Evaporate the water to crystallise the salt. Which statement about the method is correct? A The filtrate in step 2 is pure water., B The residue in step 2 is pure crystals of salt., C The solute is salt., D The solvent is a mixture of salt and water.
questions.text: Rock salt is a mixture of salt and sand. The method used to separate the sand from the salt is listed. step 1   Crush the rock salt, add to warm water and stir. step 2   Pour the mixture through a filter paper held in a funnel. step 3   Evaporate the water to crystallise the salt. Which statement about the method is correct? A The filtrate in step 2 is pure water., B The residue in step 2 is pure crystals of salt., C The solute is salt., D The solvent is a mixture of salt and water.
options: A The filtrate in step 2 is pure water.
B The residue in step 2 is pure crystals of salt.
D The solvent is a mixture of salt and water.
